ddt:Target.Name: FrontCamera_C1A_V3.19
ddt:Target.Description:
** 16/06/2016 V1 Initialisation de la base DDT de la caméra frontale C1A. Pilote : Akka (R.GUINGAND) Sce : 65616
Supplier : VALEO
** 15/02/2017 V2.0 Mise à jour de la base pour livraison SW3. Pilote : Akka (R.GUINGAND) Sce : 65616
Supplier : VALEO
** 23/06/2017 V2.2 Mise à jour de la base pour livraison SW4. Ajout des DTC Pilote : Akka (R.GUINGAND) Sce : 65616
Supplier : VALEO
** 29/08/2017 V3.0 Mise à jour de la base pour livraison SW5. Tout est inclu sauf le Diag-add-on, les paramètres SIGMA (spécifiques à Nissan) et les snapshots. Pilote : Pierre LALLEMAND-KIRCHE Sce : 65616
** 03/09/2019 V3.0 Mise à jour de la base pour l'APV Ajout des rapports de maintenabilités Pilote : Serma (C.Milapie) Sce: 65616
Supplier : VALEO
** 29/08/2017 V3.0 Mise à jour de la base pour livraison SW5. Tout est inclu sauf le Diag-add-on, les paramètres SIGMA (spécifiques à Nissan) et les snapshots. Pilote : Pierre LALLEMAND-KIRCHE Sce : 65616
Supplier : VALEO
13/10/17 : Correction des requêtes de lecture de DTC. Correction des DID $2074, $210E et $2070 pour être en conforme au SW5.
Supplier : VALEO
** 29/08/2017 V3.5 Mise à jour de la base pour livraison SW5. Modification DID country code suite à erreur. Modification paramètres AHL_AMBIENT_LIGHT_THRES_SUP et AHL_AMBIENT_LIGHT_THRES_INF dans DID $2081 suite à erreur. Pilote : Pierre LALLEMAND-KIRCHE Sce : 65616
Supplier : VALEO
** 29/11/2017 V3.6 - Guillaume Mahé Mise à jour de la base DDT suivant la DID_list V11_11 Maj de l'auto ident
14/05/2018 : V3.9 : Mise à jour selon DID_List V11_13 (sauf DID 2070 pour être cohérent avec SW 7.0/7.1). Mise à jour de l'auto-ident.
28/06/2018 : V3.10 : Mise à jour du paramètre 'param_veh.P_sys_x_LeftRightSteeringWheel' dand le DID $2013.
23/08/2018 : V3.11 : Ajout de l'auto-ident pour le SW9. 'Rapport de maintenabilité' coché pour la requête 22F1A0 pour la rendre disponible à l'usine/APV. Ajout des DID 4455, 4456 & 4457 (spécifiques Daimler et non-applicables sur ce projet) pour être en conformité avec l'OutputPermanentControlList.
31/08/2018 : V3.12 : Database is compatible with Parameter List V16 Changement de la taille du DID 210E pour être en conformité avec la spéc. Change of ID of DID 'Autofix_Activation' to be compliant with the spec. Change of coding of DID 2094 & 2095.
28/11/2018 : V3.13 : Update of snapshot $2408. Update snapshot $2402. Update auto-ident for SW11.1.
11/01/2019 : V3.14 Update of auto-ident for SW11.1 & SW11.2
18/01/2019 : V3.15 Add-on of DID F012.
08/03/2019: V3.16 Update of auto-iden for SW11.21 & SW12.1
06/07/2020 V3.18 - Sergiu FOCSENEANU Update of auto-ident for SW13.34 and SW13.36.01
05/02/2021 V3.19 - author Sergiu FOCSENEANU Database update: V3.18 + EDR screens and DIDs + TAC2 alignment screen update + SW16 autoconf 2022-5-25 Identification added by tool (CORP\py09197) => DiagVersion=0, Supplier=AFK, Soft=SW0ST17, Version=170050A712060000
2022-6-2 Identification added by tool (CORP\py09197) => DiagVersion=8, Supplier=AFK, Soft=SW0ST17, Version=170050A712060000

Item.Text: Fusion track simulation on CAN - Polar Co-ordinates
Fusion track simulation on CAN - Cartesean Co-ordinates

Data.Name: DID_Dev.LineDetectionSimulationMode
Data.Comment: Allow to activate/deactivate the LineDetectionSimulationMode. Refer to 47F 486 487

Data.Name: DID_Dev.Number of tracks
Data.Comment: Number of tracks frames sent on the CAN.

Data.Name: Mileage_snapshot.Mileage_snapshot
Data.Comment: "same coding as 'BRAKE_SPEED_R1' frame. Used for Snapshot"

Data.Name: param_veh.P_sys_x_FourWheelSteering
Data.Comment: Parameter allowing to enable (1) or deactivate (0) the rear wheel steering frame check.

Data.Name: param_veh.P_sys_x_LeftRightSteeringWheel
Data.Comment: Parameter, which indicates if the vehicle is left or right hand side

Data.Name: Configuration_finished.P_sys_x_RenaultConfigFinished
Data.Comment: Define if the ECU is configured or not. By default, this value is equal to 0.

Item.Text: ECU is not configured in Renault plant
ECU is configured in Renault plant

Data.Name: VehicleSpeedGradient.P_sys_kmh_VSMaximumGradient
Data.Comment: Vehicle speed maximal gradient for gradient failure detection

Data.Name: Radnav.Radnav_configuration_status
Data.Comment: define if the vehicle is fitted with Radio Navigation or Not. Allows to choose between Cluster and R

Data.Name: AHL.AHL_enable
Data.Comment: Activation or not of AHL function.

Data.Name: OSP_TSR.OSP_TSR_enable
Data.Comment: Activation or not of OSP/TSR function.

Data.Name: OSP_NAVSPEEDSELECTION.OSP_NAVSPEEDSELECTION
Data.Comment: For OSP function : configuration to use or not of (non-verified) speed limits from the navigation. I

Item.Text: use of only verified speed limits

Data.Name: OSP_TSR_Mode.OSP_TSR_Mode
Data.Comment: OSP/TSR function gives in this DID its current status whether it receives data from navigation (OSP

Data.Name: Active_Fault_Table.24-byte_array_of_currently_active_faults
Data.Comment: This DID is used to verify if there are any active faults present. This information is used for sys

Data.Name: Yaw_Rate.Yaw_Rate
Data.Comment: This DID read the compensated yaw rate value. The value of this reading is used by validation and sy

Data.Name: Vehicle_Speed.Vehicle_Speed
Data.Comment: This DID is used to read the current vehicle speed. The information is used by validation and verifi

Data.Name: VFP_reset_counts.VFP_Category_1_reset_count
Data.Comment: Number of VFP resets over the vehicles life time

Data.Name: Ignition_Cycles.Ignition_Cycles
Data.Comment: Number of ignition cycles the unit has had.

Data.Name: rain_strategy_selection.rain_strategy_selection
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP rain strategy : rain sensor strategy vs wiping counting

Item.Text: internal strategy based on front wiping status

Data.Name: CamAlignTM.camera_align_tm
Data.Comment: Camera Alignment Target Mode: states how many targets should be detected by the camera in alignment

Item.Text: autocalibrate mode with roll angle calibration

Data.Name: EXT_TRAFFIC_SIGN
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP : Activation / Deactivation of the overtaking sign

Item.Text: False = Extented Traffic Sign zone not active
True = Extented Traffic Sign zone active

Data.Name: OSP_SL_LINK
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP : Activation / Deactivation of the speed limter long press display message

Data.Name: SL DISPLAY MAX TIME
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP : time value for the confidence level decreasing (variant A)

Data.Name: LKA.LKA_configuration_Status
Data.Comment: Parameter flag to enable/disable the LKA function.

Data.Name: DiagAddOn_RW_LKA.Diag_add_on_LKA_ena
Data.Comment: allow to enable/disable diagaddon strategy for LKA

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: rain_strategy_selection.rain_not_active_delay
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP rain strategy : time without rain information from rain sensor

Data.Name: rain_strategy_selection.rain_active_delay
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP rain strategy : time with rain information from rain sensor

Data.Name: rain_strategy_selection.maxcountwipers
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP rain strategy : number of intermittant wiping

Data.Name: rain_strategy_selection.max_period_intermittent
Data.Comment: TSR/OSP rain strategy : time between two intermittent wiping

Data.Name: exhaustRegulationOrTypeApprovalNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the exhaust regulation or type approval number (valid for those systems which require type approval).

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemNameOrEngineType
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system name or engine type.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: programmingOperationFingerPrint
Data.Description:
The value is written by the ECU when executing the SendKey SecurityAccess service

The value can be read in boot loader mode.

Data.Name: snapshot_non_connue
Data.Comment: utilisé par Valeo mais pas dans la DID_list

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed or blocked
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures

Data.Name: Status mask
Data.Comment: Mask of DTC status to reply with 1 byte

Item.Text: Test Not Complete Since Last Clear

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: Warning info for calibration
Data.Comment: Reason for failure of the calibration

Item.Text: Left target contrast warning : check the lighting or cleaning the target, if it does not work change targets.
Right target contrast warning : check the lighting or cleaning the target, if it does not work change targets.
Right/Left target contrast warning : check the lighting or cleaning the target, if it does not work change targets.
Left target position warning (too high) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Left target position warning (too low) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Left target position warning high/low: check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Left target position warning (too much left) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.

Left target position warning (too high) and Right target contrast warning and Left target position warning (too much left) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera and check the lighting or cleaning the target, if it does not work change targets.
Left target position warning (too much right) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning (too high) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning (too low) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Left target position warning (too low) and Right target position warning (too low) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning high/low : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning (too much left) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning (too much right) : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Right target position warning left/right : check the position of the target or check the installation of the camera.
Max roll range warning (forward looking clockwise) : check the installation of the camera
Max roll range warning (forward looking counterclockwise) : check the installation of the camera
Max roll range warning clockwise/counterclockwise : check the installation of the camera
Max yaw range warning (top view clockwise) [CURRENTLY UNUSED] (Bit No 12)
Max yaw range warning (top view counterclockwise) [CURRENTLY UNUSED] (Bit No 13)
Max yaw range warning clockwise/counterclockwise : check the installation of the camera

Max pitch range warning (looking too far down) [CURRENTLY UNUSED] (Bit No 15)

Data.Name: applicationDataIdentification

This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: applicationSoftwareIdentification
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application software number(s).

The format of the application software identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Label.Text: Dctow - Camera to front axle distance (m)
Dco - Camera lens to vehicle centerline distance (m)
Dwtob - Front axle to front bumper distance (m)
Masking area in bottom side of FOV (pixels)
DID $4028 (Threshold of Distance Before Displaying the Long Press Button Message)
0 = use of only verified speed limits
Begin Mode : s (0) or m (1) selection
End Mode : s (0) or m (1) selection
Routine For SPC (angular autocalibration while driving)
STEP 2 : REQUEST ROUTINE RESULTS (until routine is complete)
Routines for Aiming (angular calibration on targets) - Normal Mode
Dh - Camera height correction from nominal value (mm)
Dt - Distance from front axle to targets board (mm)
To end Aiming Mode, a reset is necessary
STEP 2 : RESET AIMING PARAMETERS TO INITIAL
Request results until the routine is complete

Unlock the camera before launching EDR Data Erase:
4. Click "Unlock" button. VPN access required to enable Security Key Unlocking
5. Go to "Manual request" and launch 31010302, 3 replies are possible:

Be careful ! By launching EDR erase routine, all data will be erased in one step. Data concerned: images, context data, event presence, event priority and event mileage for all events recorded. In one word, everything related to EDR will be erased.
b. 7101030221 - Memory empty, no EDR data present
6. Check "EDR Presence Priority Mileage" screen if data is still present
Dt - Distance from front axle to targets board (mm)
Dh - Camera height correction from nominal value (mm)
Dctow - Distance camera to front axle (m)
Dwtob - Distance front axle to bumper (m)
Attendre ~5s avant de cliquer sur le bouton suivant
Values for Hc, Dwtob, Dctow, Vehicle width, Dco can be found in "TCA2 Guidelines" screen
